0.q: U. hordei genes encoding the calcineurin catalytic (UhCna1) and regulatory (UhCnb1) subunits were identified... Phosphatase activity resides in the catalytic subunit.

1.q: The inactive protein is a heterodimer formed by one catalytic subunit A (Cna) and one regulatory subunit B (Cnb).

2.q: Expression analyses revealed that response to salt stress involved the induction of membrane ATPase genes UhEna1 and UhEna2, which were regulated through the calcineurin pathway.

3.q: Expression analyses revealed that response to salt stress involved the induction of membrane ATPase genes UhEna1 and UhEna2, which were regulated through the calcineurin pathway.

4.q: Calcineurin pathway is involved in cell-wall integrity. Mutants were not viable when either 0.002% SDS, Congo red, Calcofluor white, or 0.09 mM caffeine were present in the culture media.

5.q: Calcineurin pathway is involved in cell-wall integrity. Mutants were not viable when either 0.002% SDS, Congo red, Calcofluor white, or 0.09 mM caffeine were present in the culture media.

6.q: U. hordei genes encoding the calcineurin catalytic (UhCna1) and regulatory (UhCnb1) subunits were identified... Phosphatase activity resides in the catalytic subunit.

7.q: Upregulation of UhFKS1, a 1,3-β-D-glucan synthase gene, correlated with the increased amount of 1,3-β-D-glucan in the calcineurin mutants grown under salt stress.
